problem: Mutation approval.approve trigger has no isPending reference — button not disabled while pending, double-submit possible.
fix: Add disabled={mutation.isPending} to the trigger element and render a loading indicator.

problem: Destructive mutation approval.reject fires without a confirmation dialog in this file.
fix: Wrap the trigger in <AlertDialog> from @/components/ui/alert-dialog and only call mutate from AlertDialogAction onClick.

triage.reason: Handler input requires a non-empty `comment: string`. The trigger opens a Popover with a textarea + 10-char-minimum validation; mutate only fires after the user types and confirms. The required-input gate is functionally equivalent to an AlertDialog confirmation.

problem: Mutation classificationDashboard.exportMarketCsv trigger has no isPending reference — button not disabled while pending, double-submit possible.
fix: Add disabled={mutation.isPending} to the trigger element and render a loading indicator.

triage.reason: Button has `disabled={isPending}` where `isPending = mutation.status === 'pending'` (line 48). The detector only matches the literal `<var>.isPending` property; the status-comparison alias is functionally identical.

problem: Mutation classification.saveAnswer trigger has no isPending reference — button not disabled while pending, double-submit possible.
fix: Add disabled={mutation.isPending} to the trigger element and render a loading indicator.

triage.reason: Autosave pattern — `onMutate` flips a separate `autosaveStatus` state to 'saving' so the UI can render saving/saved/error indicators around the wizard. No discrete Button to disable; commitAnswer fires on every input change.

problem: Mutation payment.importStatement trigger has no isPending reference — button not disabled while pending, double-submit possible.
fix: Add disabled={mutation.isPending} to the trigger element and render a loading indicator.

triage.reason: Trigger is a `<input type="file">` whose onChange flips a state machine to `step = 'parsing'` before calling `mutate`. UI conditionally renders parsing/results/error views from `step`; there is no static Button to disable because the file input itself cannot be disabled mid-upload.

problem: Mutation payment.updateItemStatus trigger has no isPending reference — button not disabled while pending, double-submit possible.
fix: Add disabled={mutation.isPending} to the trigger element and render a loading indicator.

problem: Mutation portal.updateNotificationPreference trigger has no isPending reference — button not disabled while pending, double-submit possible.
fix: Add disabled={mutation.isPending} to the trigger element and render a loading indicator.

triage.reason: Mutation uses optimistic-update (`onMutate` writes new value to query cache + `onSettled` refetches). Disabling the Switch while pending would defeat the optimistic UX — toggle feedback is instant by design.

problem: Mutation portal.logout has onError but does not call toast.error — user sees no error feedback.
fix: Inside the existing onError handler, call toast.error(err.message) (or a translated message).

triage.reason: Logout failures are intentionally swallowed — an explicit no-op onError exists so the client clears local state regardless. Server-side errors are non-actionable for an already-logged-out user.

problem: Mutation portal.logout has no onSuccess handler — UI never updates or confirms.
fix: Add onSuccess: () => { toast.success(...); queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ... }); }

triage.reason: onSuccess is intentionally absent — logout always redirects via router.replace immediately after mutateAsync resolves. There is no UI to update because the page unmounts.

problem: Mutation gdpr.requestErasure succeeds without invalidating queries — stale UI until reload.
fix: Call queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: trpc.<relatedQuery>.queryOptions(...).queryKey }) in onSuccess.

triage.reason: Erasure soft-deletes the entire org — there are no queries left to invalidate because the user session ends and the next request hits the deleted-org branch. Intentional no-op.

problem: Mutation integration.disconnectGeneric trigger has no isPending reference — button not disabled while pending, double-submit possible.
fix: Add disabled={mutation.isPending} to the trigger element and render a loading indicator.

triage.reason: Three useMutation hooks (genericDisconnect / jiraDisconnect / ksefDisconnect) feed a single `disconnectMutation` alias picked by provider. The single AlertDialogAction trigger uses `disabled={disconnectMutation.isPending}`, so the active route's `isPending` IS bound — the detector just doesn't follow the conditional-alias pattern.

problem: Mutation workflow.seedStarterTemplates trigger has no isPending reference — button not disabled while pending, double-submit possible.
fix: Add disabled={mutation.isPending} to the trigger element and render a loading indicator.

triage.reason: Mutation auto-fires from a `useEffect` (line 144) on first visit when `templates.length === 0` and `seedAttempted.current` is false. There is no user-triggered Button to disable; the `seedAttempted` ref prevents double-fire.

problem: Mutation zatca.runComplianceChecks succeeds without invalidating queries — stale UI until reload.
fix: Call queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: trpc.<relatedQuery>.queryOptions(...).queryKey }) in onSuccess.

triage.reason: ZATCA wizard advances by re-querying getOnboardingState between steps, not by invalidating. UI is conditional on step state held in the parent form, so cache invalidation would be redundant.

problem: Mutation zatca.requestComplianceCsid succeeds without invalidating queries — stale UI until reload.
fix: Call queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: trpc.<relatedQuery>.queryOptions(...).queryKey }) in onSuccess.

triage.reason: Same as zatca.runComplianceChecks — wizard step transitions drive the UI, not query invalidation.

problem: Destructive mutation approval.reject fires without a confirmation dialog in this file.
fix: Wrap the trigger in <AlertDialog> from @/components/ui/alert-dialog and only call mutate from AlertDialogAction onClick.

triage.reason: Hook file cannot host an AlertDialog itself; confirmation lives in the consuming components (approvals/page.tsx, approval-queue/side-panel.tsx) which open the comment-collecting Popover before calling the hook.

problem: Mutation user.pins.toggle has no onSuccess handler — UI never updates or confirms.
fix: Add onSuccess: () => { toast.success(...); queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ... }); }

triage.reason: Uses optimistic-update pattern: onMutate writes the new pin state to the query cache, onSettled refetches. No onSuccess needed because the cache is mutated before the round-trip.

problem: Destructive mutation workflow.deleteTemplate fires without a confirmation dialog in this file.
fix: Wrap the trigger in <AlertDialog> from @/components/ui/alert-dialog and only call mutate from AlertDialogAction onClick.

triage.reason: Hook cannot host the dialog. Consumer templates-table.tsx wraps the delete trigger in <AlertDialog> at lines 354-372 — confirmation is enforced at the call site.

problem: Mutation document.uploadNewVersion has no onSuccess handler — UI never updates or confirms.
fix: Add onSuccess: () => { toast.success(...); queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ... }); }

triage.reason: Consumed via mutateAsync() inside an async pipeline. The caller awaits the result, runs a subsequent mutate (confirmUpload), and handles toast/invalidation at the end of the pipeline — not inside this mutation's options.

problem: Mutation document.confirmUpload has no onSuccess handler — UI never updates or confirms.
fix: Add onSuccess: () => { toast.success(...); queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ... }); }

triage.reason: Same as document.uploadNewVersion — terminal step of an async pipeline. The caller's awaited handler does the post-success work (toast + invalidation), not the mutation's onSuccess.
